body:
## Goal
Ship a persistent, multi-user JSON REST API for projects and tasks with token auth, built on Node's core http server with no external web framework.

## Slicing
1. Store layer: on-disk SQLite (or equivalent) schema for users/projects/tasks with a cascade from project→tasks; open/create the db file idempotently. 2. HTTP plumbing: core-http server on PORT, JSON body parsing with size/parse guards, a tiny path-param router, and a top-level try/catch that maps errors to JSON 4xx/5xx so no request crashes the process. 3. Auth: register (hash+salt password, unique username, return {id,username}), login (verify, return bearer token), and a bearer-token middleware that resolves the caller. 4. Projects handlers (owner-scoped CRUD + cascade delete). 5. Tasks handlers (create with optional assignee, paginated+filtered list, patch, delete; all gated on parent-project ownership). 6. Tests exercising the full happy path + auth/ownership/robustness edges; wire `npm start`.

## Spec
Runtime: Node.js ESM, no external web framework (Node core `http`/`node:http` only). A minimal dependency for the on-disk store is permitted (e.g. `better-sqlite3` or `node:sqlite`); data MUST persist to an on-disk file and survive process restart — no in-memory-only store. Server binds `process.env.PORT` (default 3000). `npm start` launches it.

Auth: POST /auth/register {username,password} creates a user, returns {id, username} (never the password/hash); usernames unique. POST /auth/login {username,password} returns {token} — an opaque/bearer token. All /projects and /projects/:projectId/tasks endpoints require a valid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header; missing/invalid → 401. Passwords stored hashed+salted, never plaintext.

Projects (owner-scoped): POST /projects {name} → {id,name,ownerId,createdAt}. GET /projects → JSON array of the caller's own projects only. GET /projects/:id and DELETE /projects/:id → 404 when the id is not an existing project owned by the caller (no cross-user leakage). DELETE cascades: the project's tasks are removed too.

Tasks (nested under an owned project): POST /projects/:projectId/tasks {title, assigneeId?} → {id,title,projectId,assigneeId,createdAt,done:false}; assigneeId is optional and may reference another existing user. GET /projects/:projectId/tasks → {items,total,page,pageSize} paginated via ?page & ?pageSize (sane defaults + bounds), with optional ?done=true|false filter. PATCH /projects/:projectId/tasks/:taskId {title?,done?} → the updated task. DELETE /projects/:projectId/tasks/:taskId removes it. Task routes are reachable only by the owning user of the parent project; otherwise 404.

Robustness invariant: no request crashes the process. Malformed JSON, wrong types, missing fields, unknown routes, and bad pagination params yield a JSON error with an appropriate 4xx (400/401/404) status, never a 500 from an unhandled throw.

## Changes
- `package.json` — refactors-existing
- `src/server.js` — creates
- `src/router.js` — creates
- `src/store.js` — creates
- `src/auth.js` — creates
- `src/handlers/auth.js` — creates
- `src/handlers/projects.js` — creates
- `src/handlers/tasks.js` — creates
- `test/api.test.js` — creates

## Acceptance
- [ ] AC-1: `npm start` boots the server on process.env.PORT (default 3000) using Node core http and no external web framework; a fresh clone with the on-disk db absent starts cleanly and creates the store file.
- [ ] AC-2: POST /auth/register with {username,password} returns {id,username} (no password/hash echoed); a duplicate username is rejected with a 4xx rather than creating a second user.
- [ ] AC-3: POST /auth/login with correct credentials returns {token}; wrong credentials return 401; the token authorizes subsequent requests via `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.
- [ ] AC-4: All /projects and /projects/:projectId/tasks requests without a valid bearer token return 401.
- [ ] AC-5: POST /projects returns {id,name,ownerId,createdAt}; GET /projects returns a JSON array containing only the caller's own projects; GET/DELETE /projects/:id on a non-owned or nonexistent id returns 404.
- [ ] AC-6: DELETE /projects/:id removes the project and its tasks (the tasks are no longer retrievable afterward).
- [ ] AC-7: POST /projects/:projectId/tasks returns {id,title,projectId,assigneeId,createdAt,done:false} and accepts an optional assigneeId referencing another user.
- [ ] AC-8: GET /projects/:projectId/tasks returns {items,total,page,pageSize}, honors ?page & ?pageSize, and filters by ?done=true|false.
- [ ] AC-9: PATCH /projects/:projectId/tasks/:taskId updates title and/or done and returns the updated task; DELETE removes it.
- [ ] AC-10: Data written before a restart is still present after the server is stopped and restarted.
- [ ] AC-11: Malformed JSON bodies, wrong-typed fields, unknown routes, and bad pagination params return a JSON 4xx error and never crash the server process (no unhandled 500 from a thrown error).

## Verify
- npm start (validate)
- node --test test/api.test.js (e2e)

## Non-Goals
- No web UI or HTML rendering.
- No password reset, email verification, refresh tokens, or role/permission tiers beyond owner-scoping.
- No external web framework (Express/Fastify/etc.) and no in-memory-only store.
- No real-time (WebSocket) or task comment/attachment features.

<!-- meta: {"reason_to_exist":"A signed-in user needs to manage projects and their tasks over a durable, restart-safe JSON API without pulling in a web framework."} -->

<!-- plan-story: 9573c179bc9b8c22 -->

title: Multi-user project & task management REST API (Node ESM, on-disk store)
